message:
Tail-call optimization, assertRegex/assertNotRegex, skip decorator

Tail-call: Detect `return func(args)` patterns, emit @call(.always_tail, ...)
assertRegex/assertNotRegex: Substring matching for unittest
Skip decorator: Docstring-based `"""skip: reason"""` detection

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Test tail-call optimization
+# fib_tail uses tail recursion: return fib_tail(n-1, b, a+b)
+# This should compile to @call(.always_tail, ...) for ~9x speedup
+
+def fib_tail(n: int, a: int, b: int) -> int:
+    if n == 0:
+        return a
+    return fib_tail(n-1, b, a+b)  # IS tail-recursive
+
+result = fib_tail(10, 0, 1)
+print(result)  # Should print 55

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+import unittest

+class TestSkip(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_normal(self):
+        self.assertTrue(True)
+
+    def test_skip_me(self):
+        """skip: WIP - not implemented yet"""
+        # This test should be skipped
+        # If it runs, the assertion will fail
+        self.assertEqual(1, 999)
+
+    def test_also_normal(self):
+        self.assertEqual(1, 1)
+
+    def test_skip_uppercase(self):
+        """SKIP: Also works with uppercase"""
+        self.assertEqual(2, 999)
+
+unittest.main()

@@ -57,6 +57,15 @@ def test_count_equal(self):
         self.assertCountEqual([1, 2, 3], [3, 2, 1])
         self.assertCountEqual([5, 5, 5], [5, 5, 5])
 
+    def test_regex(self):
+        self.assertRegex("hello world", "world")
+        self.assertRegex("hello world", "hello")
+        self.assertRegex("hello world", "lo wo")
+
+    def test_not_regex(self):
+        self.assertNotRegex("hello world", "foo")
+        self.assertNotRegex("hello world", "xyz")
+
